title: Planchonella hookeri
intro: Planchonella hookeri, commonly known as Hooker's Planchonella, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to the tropical forests of Australia and New Guinea. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and the treatment of infections. The bioactive constituents of Planchonella hookeri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including a tincture and a decoction, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory infections.

It is native to New Caledonia and is a part of the genus Planchonella, which consists of several tropical and subtropical species.
